code_usability.detail: No executable code generated; Mode A writing skill

reliability.note: Handles vague topics by requesting clarification; no strict minimum-topic threshold defined

agent_usability.note: Feedback design slightly underspecified for what confirmation to produce after each step

agent_specific.note: Description includes natural trigger phrases; composability good as upstream tool

inputs.label: RCT search strategy for metformin and cardiovascular outcomes in T2DM

assertions.text: Provides line-by-line breakdown AND single copy-paste string
Each concept group covers both MeSH and free-text synonyms
Study-type filter justified and correct syntax applied
No fabricated MeSH terms; verification note included
Output is copy-paste ready without manual editing

inputs.label: Embase adaptation with Emtree terms for same clinical question

assertions.text: Emtree terms substituted for MeSH terms with /exp notation
Title/abstract syntax .ti,ab. correctly applied
Differences from PubMed query explicitly noted
No fabricated Emtree terms; uncertainty noted where applicable
Cross-database adaptation explained clearly

inputs.label: Systematic review search strategy for gut microbiome and mental health

assertions.text: SR/MA publication type filter included
Recall-oriented approach used with broad synonym coverage
Sensitivity vs specificity trade-off discussed
No fabricated search results or hit counts
MeSH Browser verification note present
Vague topic correctly not converted into a direct query
Clarification questions asked before proceeding
Scope narrowing guidance provided
No fabricated search strategy from insufficient input
Example sub-topic directions offered to help user narrow

inputs.label: Multi-concept PICO + WoS adaptation + date filter + animal exclusion

assertions.text: WoS TS= field tag correctly applied
Animal exclusion filter using correct syntax
Date filter syntax correct for target database
Cross-database consistency maintained
No fabricated terms or results

key_strengths: Cross-database adaptation section (Embase/WoS/Cochrane) provides unique multi-platform coverage
Hard rule against fabricating MeSH terms with mandatory verification note is a strong integrity safeguard
Quality self-checklist ensures completeness before output is finalized
Description uses natural trigger language covering all major user scenarios

recommendations.title: No minimum topic specificity gate before query generation
recommendations.problem: When a user provides only a very broad topic (e.g., 'cancer'), the skill asks for clarification but does not define the minimum specificity required before proceeding.
recommendations.root_cause: Clarification guidance exists but no formal minimum-input threshold is defined.
recommendations.fix: Add a minimum-specificity rule: if neither a disease subtype, mechanism, intervention, OR outcome is provided, request at minimum one PICO element before building any query.

recommendations.title: Feedback design underspecifies what to confirm after each step
recommendations.problem: The 6-step workflow produces good output but does not specify what confirmation or intermediate output the agent should surface after, e.g., Step 2 (MeSH mapping) before proceeding to Step 3 (Boolean construction).
recommendations.root_cause: Workflow is delivery-oriented rather than iterative.
recommendations.fix: Add an optional interim check-in at Step 2: present the MeSH mapping table to the user and confirm accuracy before proceeding to Step 3 for complex multi-concept queries.
